_validation_note: tJoin joins a main input with a single lookup input on one or more key-column pairs. use_inner_join=false (default) is a LEFT OUTER join that KEEPS every main row (unmatched rows also route to the reject output); use_inner_join=true drops unmatched main rows from main. join_key is REQUIRED and non-empty; each item needs both input_column (main side) and lookup_column (lookup side). Null keys NEVER match (SQL/Talend semantics), and the lookup side is first-match deduplicated on the join key. IMPORTANT: NO lookup columns are added to the output unless use_lookup_cols=true AND lookup_cols lists them ({output_column, lookup_column}); by default only the main-input columns pass through. case_sensitive=true (default) compares keys exactly; false lowercases both sides before matching. Unmatched main rows go to the reject output; die_on_error=true (default) raises on failure, false degrades to an empty main plus the full main input as reject.
